summary: Kenneth Suna demonstrates a weekly covered call strategy targeting volatile stocks to generate consistent premium income. He analyzes three high-volatility stocks (BBAI, Archer Aviation, Joby Aviation) with 1,000-share positions, comparing returns on premium income alone versus total profit including stock appreciation, and shows how position sizing can optimize dollar returns for fixed capital.
takeaways: Sell covered calls on 1,000-share positions (10 contracts per position)
Target weekly expirations for faster income cycles
BBAI example: $7 entry, $7.50 strike, $400 premium = 5.6% weekly return
Archer Aviation example: $10.78 entry, $11 strike, $340 premium = 3.1% weekly return
Joby Aviation example: $12.33 entry, $12.50 strike, $500 premium = 4.0% weekly return
Choose strike prices 5-10% above current stock price for high premium
Accept capped upside: "I'm not here to make all the money, just some of it"
Reposition sizing for better returns: same $12k in BBAI (1,700 shares) yields $680 premium vs $500 in Joby

strategy_candidates.change: Prioritize high-volatility stocks (>300% annual moves like BBAI) in weekly covered call rotation to maximize premium per capital deployed
strategy_candidates.evidence: BBAI's 359% one-year gain enabled 5.6% weekly returns vs 3-4% for lower-volatility names; Kenneth explicitly hunts for "volatile stocks that also pay high premiums" and uses this to generate $600/week income

title: I’m going to make $600 Next Week Selling COVERED CALLS
